name: storify

metadata.name: storify
metadata.summary: A lightweight database system for Python

documentation:
# Storify

Storify is a dead-simple, lightweight Python database library that uses msgpack for efficient data serialization. 

It provides a framework for data storage and retrieval, with an optional ORM model for interaction with data. 

Storify supports automatic backups and error handling, ensuring data integrity and reliability. 

## Features

- Create and manage multiple msgpack-based databases with ease.
- Lightweight ORM-esque model for easy interaction with data.
- Automatic backups and data flushing to prevent data loss.
- Built-in error handling to automatically recover from database corruption and loading issues.
- Configurable save intervals for optimized performance.

## Installation

```bash
pip install storify
```

## Usage

Here's a simplified example of how to use the `storify` package:

```python
from storify import Storify

# Initialize Storify
storify = Storify(root="example_data")
db_name = "example_db"

# Check if the database exists
if storify.db_exists(db_name):
    print(f"Database '{db_name}' exists. It will be loaded.")

# Initialize a new database
 db = storify.get_db(name=db_name)

# Add some data to the database 
db["key1"] = "value1" 
db["key2"] = "value2"

# Get some data from the database
print(f"key1: {db['key1']}")
print(f"key2: {db['key2']}")

# Flush all databases (force save)
storify.flush()
```

For more complete examples, including how to use the ORM, please check the `examples` folder.

methods.name: storify.model.Model._key
documentation:
This method is used to get the key for the model.
By default, it returns the class name with double underscores prepended and appended.

methods.name: storify.model.Model._keyname
documentation:
This method is used to get the key name for the model.
By default, it returns the class name with double underscores prepended and appended.

methods.name: storify.model.Model._to_dict
documentation:
This method is used to convert the model instance to a dictionary.
By default, it returns a dictionary of all the instance's attributes
that do not start with an underscore.

methods.name: storify.model.Model._from_dict
documentation:
This method is used to convert a dictionary to a model instance.
By default, it updates the instance's attributes with the values from
the dictionary.

methods.name: storify.model.Model._deserialize
documentation:
This method is used to convert a dictionary to a model instance.
By default, it uses the _from_dict method to convert the dictionary to an instance.

methods.name: storify.database.Database.load
documentation:
Load the database from a file.

```
param path

The path to the database file. If not provided, defaults to the standard path based on the database name.

type path

str, optional

raises DatabaseLoadError

If the database cannot be loaded from the main file or any backups.
```

Typically, you don't need to call this yourself. This method attempts to load the database from the specified file.
If the main file is corrupted, it tries to load from available backups. If all attempts fail, it raises a DatabaseLoadError.

methods.name: storify.database.Database.flush
documentation:
Flush the database to disk.

Writes all pending changes in the database to disk storage. Creates a temporary file,
writes the data, and then safely moves it to the final location. Before writing, creates
a backup of the existing database file if one exists.

If the database has been marked as destroyed or defunct, returns without performing
any operations.

```
raises IOError

If there is an error writing the data to disk, typically due to insufficient storage space
```

methods.name: storify.database.Database.close
documentation:
Close the database.

Flushes the database to disk and marks it as defunct. After closing,
the database can no longer be used.

methods.name: storify.database.Database.destroy
documentation:
Destroy the database.

Deletes the database file from disk. This operation cannot be undone,
but backups are preserved.

methods.name: storify.database.backups.Backups.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the Backups instance.

```
param db

Database instance to manage backups for

type db

Database
```

methods.name: storify.database.backups.Backups.backup
documentation:
Create a backup of the database.

Creates a new backup file with an incremented ID and removes oldest backups
if the maximum number of backups is exceeded.

methods.name: storify.database.backups.Backups.db_path
documentation:
Get the path to the main database file.

```
return

Full path to the database file

rtype

str
```
def restore(self, backup_id):
path = self.get_path_of_backup(backup_id)

methods.name: storify.database.backups.Backups.backup_path
documentation:
Get the path to the backup directory.

```
return

Full path to the backup directory

rtype

str
```

methods.name: storify.database.backups.Backups.list
documentation:
List all backups of the database.

Creates backup directory if it doesn't exist.

```
return

List of backup IDs sorted in descending order

rtype

list[int]
```

methods.name: storify.database.backups.Backups.latest
documentation:
Get the most recent backup ID.

```
return

ID of the most recent backup, or 0 if no backups exist

rtype

int
```

methods.name: storify.database.backups.Backups.last
documentation:
Get the oldest backup of the database.

```
return

ID of the oldest backup

rtype

int
```

methods.name: storify.database.backups.Backups.get_path_of_backup
documentation:
Get the full path of a specific backup.

```
param backup_id

ID of the backup

type backup_id

int

return

Full path to the backup file

rtype

str
```

methods.name: storify.Storify.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the Storify instance.

```
param root

The root directory where databases will be stored

type root

str

default root

"data"

param save_interval

The interval in seconds for automatic saving of databases

type save_interval

int

default save_interval

60

param log

Logger instance for logging messages

type log

DummyLogger

default log

None

param models

A list of model classes to be used with the Storify instance

type models

list

default models

[]
```

methods.name: storify.Storify.get_db
documentation:
Get or create a database instance.

```
param name

Name of the database

type name

str

param root

Initial root data structure for new database

type root

dict

default root

{}

return

Database instance

rtype

Database
```

methods.name: storify.Storify.db_exists
documentation:
Check if a database exists.

```
param name

Name of the database to check

type name

str

return

True if database exists, False otherwise

rtype

bool
```

methods.name: storify.Storify.rename_db
documentation:
Rename a database file.

:::{warning}
Dangerous to use if database is currently open!
:::

```
param old_name

Current name of the database

type old_name

str

param new_name

New name for the database

type new_name

str
```

methods.name: storify.Storify.remove_db
documentation:
Remove a database file.

:::{warning}
May be ineffective if database is currently open!
:::

```
param name

Name of the database to remove

type name

str
```

methods.name: storify.Storify.tick
documentation:
Tick all open databases.

Flushes databases to disk if they haven't been flushed recently based on save_interval.

```
param force

Force flush all databases regardless of last flush time

type force

bool

default force

False
```

methods.name: storify.Storify.flush
documentation:
Flush all open databases to disk immediately.

Forces an immediate flush of all active databases by calling tick with force=True.
